name: cancel_workstream
description: Cancel in-flight generation on a child and unblock any pending approval / plan review. Does not close or delete — state moves to `idle`, so follow up with send_to_workstream or pair with close_workstream to retire. Drops tokens spent on the in-flight call. Use when a child is stuck on an approval you no longer want to grant, gone off the rails on a long thinking turn, or just needs a fresh prompt. Returns `{ws_id, cancelled, status, dropped?}`. `dropped` is included when the node captured forensics: it always carries `was_running: bool` (true mid-generation, false if the cancel arrived idle), and conditionally `pending_approval: {tool_names: [str], call_id: str}` when an approval was pending and `queued_messages: {count: int, first_preview: str}` when messages were queued behind the in-flight call. Read `was_running` first to judge whether the cancel actually interrupted work.
